Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Show HN: I built a tiny LLM to demystify how language models work 111 upvotes, 8 comments
  2. Nanocode: The best Claude Code that $200 can buy in pure JAX on TPUs 170 upvotes, 24 comments
  3. A tail-call interpreter in (nightly) Rust 134 upvotes, 22 comments
  4. Computational Physics (2nd Edition) (2025) 109 upvotes, 17 comments
  5. Wavelets on Graphs via Spectral Graph Theory (2009) 29 upvotes, 2 comments
  6. Show HN: Gemma Gem – AI model embedded in a browser – no API keys, no cloud 21 upvotes, 1 comments
  7. The Mechanics of Steins Gate (2023) [pdf] 54 upvotes, 9 comments
  8. Gemma 4 on iPhone 463 upvotes, 127 comments
  9. Running Gemma 4 locally with LM Studio's new headless CLI and Claude Code 208 upvotes, 55 comments
  10. Eight years of wanting, three months of building with AI 663 upvotes, 208 comments
  11. Show HN: Modo – I built an open-source alternative to Kiro, Cursor, and Windsurf 17 upvotes, 2 comments
  12. Show HN: Mdarena – Benchmark your Claude.md against your own PRs 13 upvotes, 1 comments
  13. Friendica – A Decentralized Social Network 138 upvotes, 49 comments
  14. Caveman: Why use many token when few token do trick 717 upvotes, 313 comments
  15. LÖVE: 2D Game Framework for Lua 224 upvotes, 91 comments
  16. Employers use your personal data to figure out the lowest salary you'll accept 102 upvotes, 38 comments
  17. Music for Programming 119 upvotes, 51 comments
  18. Copilot is 'for entertainment purposes only', per Microsoft's terms of use 33 upvotes, 10 comments
  19. Microsoft hasn't had a coherent GUI strategy since Petzold 252 upvotes, 142 comments
  20. Sheets Spreadsheets in Your Terminal 16 upvotes, 4 comments
  21. OpenAI's fall from grace as investors race to Anthropic 105 upvotes, 60 comments
  22. Artemis II crew see first glimpse of far side of Moon [video] 434 upvotes, 332 comments
  23. Stamp It All Programs Must Report Their Version – Michael Stapelberg 4 upvotes, 0 comments
  24. Why Switzerland has 25 Gbit internet and America doesn't 280 upvotes, 226 comments
  25. Show HN: YouTube search barely works, I made a search form with advanced filters 95 upvotes, 68 comments
  26. Apex Protocol – An open MCP-based standard for AI agent trading 10 upvotes, 3 comments
  27. In Japan, the robot isn't coming for your job; it's filling the one nobody wants 135 upvotes, 149 comments
  28. Recall – local multimodal semantic search for your files 12 upvotes, 8 comments
  29. Endian wars and anti-portability: this again? 21 upvotes, 19 comments
